medications: Naproxen 220 mg as needed for knee pain
health.mental_health: High stress and poor sleep related to her teenager’s escalating behavior at home and repeated school meetings; occasional panic-like episodes before phone calls from the school, but she has not started formal therapy yet.

Diabetes education visit with a clinic dietitian (6 weeks ago)
Podiatry visit for foot numbness and shoe/insoles recommendations (2 months ago)

finance.debt: Owes on a used SUV loan (~$9k remaining) and has revolving credit card debt (~$4k) that grew during months of missed shifts and fees tied to her teen’s school/behavioral program requirements; she is also behind one month on a medical payment plan for lab work.

Purchased a refurbished Chromebook ($189) after the school required a device for make-up coursework
Pharmacy copays totaling ~$48 for blood pressure and diabetes medications
Payment to a towing company ($165) after the family car was left illegally parked during a school incident

relationships: Daughter: Mei Zhang (16), currently struggling with truancy, vaping, and frequent conflicts at home

social.social_media_activity: Keeps a low profile; mostly reads neighborhood posts and auto-repair tips in local Facebook groups, rarely posts photos. She has muted school-related parent groups to avoid gossip and triggering comments.

Talking with the school about a possible IEP/504 evaluation and disciplinary record concerns
Confiding in a friend about a recent incident where her teen left the house overnight without permission
Debating with her ex-husband about whether to involve juvenile services or file an incident report for property damage at home

regular_appointments: Blood pressure self-check at a pharmacy kiosk every Sunday morning
Monthly diabetes supply pickup and medication refill (last week of each month)
Biweekly school meetings/calls with counselor or dean (usually Tues or Thurs afternoons)
Knee physical therapy exercises at home 3x/week (evenings)

Follow-up appointment for A1C and lipid panel review (in 3 weeks, 9:10am)

schedule.daily_routine: Up at 5:45am to check fasting glucose and pack lunches; works full day on the shop floor; evenings are split between cooking a lower-carb dinner, supervising homework, monitoring her teen’s whereabouts, and returning calls from the school. Sleep is often interrupted by late-night arguments or worry.

professional.company: North Loop Auto & Tire (independent repair shop in Minneapolis)
projects: Diagnosing intermittent misfire and emissions issues on a 2016 Ford Escape
Brake and suspension overhaul for a fleet of delivery vans
Training a new lube tech on inspection checklists and customer communication
professional.performance_issues: Has had a few late arrivals and stepped away to take urgent calls from the school; her manager informally warned her about missed billable hours during weeks with multiple school incidents, which could affect her bonus.

Service writer: Tasha Nguyen, helps juggle scheduling when Jessica needs to leave early
Coworker: Mike Havel, occasionally makes insensitive comments about 'kids these days,' adding stress

Walking around Lake Nokomis when weather allows
Cooking simple Chinese-American meals adapted for diabetes (less rice, more vegetables)
Listening to true-crime podcasts during commutes
habits: Checks blood pressure and glucose regularly and logs results in a phone app
Keeps the house quieter and locks up keys/valuables after past incidents with her teen
Drinks 1–2 glasses of wine on some nights to unwind after conflicts at home
Uses a nicotine vape occasionally despite trying to quit, especially after stressful school calls
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom duplex in South Minneapolis; lives with her 16-year-old daughter. The home environment is tense at times, with rules about curfews and phone access frequently contested.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Tries to follow a lower-sodium, lower-carb diet for hypertension and diabetes; meal planning is inconsistent when her teen refuses family dinners or when Jessica works late.
